description: In Java/JVM, run the package-local gate, fix the first owned diagnostic, rerun, then broaden.
body: In Java/JVM, the fastest path through errors is to let javac at the configured --release with -Xlint warnings and SpotBugs, Error Prone, and Checkstyle reports drive one fix at a time rather than guessing. Use when the Maven/Gradle module task (mvn -pl / gradle :module:test) with the right JDK toolchain or the analyzers report multiple diagnostics. Do not batch speculative edits across files, and avoid suppressing diagnostics to move on. Actions: inspect the package, run the gate, fix only the first javac error or the root-cause stack-trace frame in your package, rerun the module's JUnit suite on the affected classpath, then widen to the Maven/Gradle module task (mvn -pl / gradle :module:test) with the right JDK toolchain for the whole package. Validation signals include a shrinking diagnostic count, a passing focused test, and a clean package-wide gate. Risk boundary: if a diagnostic implies a design or API change, stop and escalate instead of forcing the gate green.
